title: Deploy Cloudflare dispatcher for reliable cron
description:
The GH Actions free-tier scheduled workflows fire at ~6% of the configured rate. The cf-dispatcher Worker fixes this.

Steps:
1. cd cf-dispatcher && pnpm install
2. pnpm exec wrangler login (as MaroMushii CF account)
3. just deploy-dispatcher
4. just set-dispatcher-token  — paste a fine-grained PAT scoped to MaroMushii/Khorshid (Actions: read+write)
5. Verify in CF dashboard: Worker > Triggers > Cron Triggers shows */5

Monitor: wrangler tail khorshid-mirror-dispatcher to confirm dispatches are landing.
notes: Started. Ops-only — no branch. Wrangler auth expired; re-login required before deploy. Plan: .beads/scratch/Khorshid-6p0.md

title: Add more Iranian news channels to mirror
description:
Expand channels.json beyond the initial 9.

Current list: bbcpersian, dwpersian, farsi_iranwire, farsivoa, hranews, iranintltv, mamlekate, radiofarda, sahamnewsorg

Candidates to evaluate and add:
- manotoofficial (Manoto TV)
- kalameh_ir
- amnestyfarsi
- hra_news (human rights)
- iranhumanrights
- roozonline

Process: verify each channel is active, has consistent posts, not a spam/disinformation vector. Add to channels.json via PR.

title: Private rooms — invite-only encrypted group discussion
description:
Post-MVP. Small-group encrypted rooms separate from public news.

Model (inherited from Khorshid):
- Room key generated at creation, shared via QR code or Bluetooth pairing
- Invite bundle: { repo, pat, key, name } → base64 → QR
- Each private room is a separate private GitHub repo
- Messages stored as encrypted comments on a GitHub Issue (like Khorshid)

Differences from Khorshid:
- Not limited to 4 people — any size group with the invite bundle
- Room creator holds the PAT; others use it anonymously
- Supports the same message types as public rooms (text, image, vote, reaction)

Depends on: identity bead, comment system bead

title: PAT pool — anonymous write tokens for public users
description:
Users don't have GitHub accounts. The app ships donated PATs for writing to Firestore and GitHub.

Design:
- PAT pool stored in app binary + a self-updating credential file in the repo (raw.githubusercontent.com fetch on launch)
- PATs are fine-grained, scoped to write-only on public repos
- App picks a random PAT per write operation
- If a PAT is rate-limited or revoked, the app tries the next one

Risk if PATs are extracted from binary:
- Attacker can write unsigned garbage (filtered: signature check fails)
- Cannot impersonate anyone (no private key)
- Cannot read private rooms (no AES key)

Rotation: update the credential file in the repo. Apps pick it up on next launch.

title: Today's Highlights — daily curated feed sorted by importance votes
description:
The core editorial surface of Khorshid.

Two sections per day:
1. Highlights — top-N posts by importance vote count (the 'this matters' signal)
2. All posts — chronological scroll of everything else

GH Actions aggregator (runs every minute):
- Reads Firestore vote counts per post
- Merges with mirror snapshot for the day
- Writes feed/{day}.json to export branch sorted by importance desc
- Apps read this as the fallback path when Firestore is unreachable

UI:
- Today's date is the default view on launch
- Yesterday / date picker to navigate back
- Highlights section pinned at top with visual distinction
- Post card shows: headline, channel, importance vote count, comment count

title: Comment system — write and read comments via Firestore
description:
Implement commenting under news posts.

Write path:
- User writes comment → sign with Ed25519 → AES-GCM encrypt with room key → pick PAT from pool → write to Firestore + GitHub
- Comment document: { id (sha256 of payload), sender_pubkey, payload (ciphertext), nonce, sig, sent_at, reply_to? }

Read path:
- Firestore real-time listener on posts/{postId}/comments
- Decrypt with room key, verify signature
- Display as threaded list under each post (reply_to for nesting)

PAT pool needed for write path — block on that bead or implement with single hardcoded PAT for dev.

title: Voting system — upvote posts and comments via Firestore
description:
Implement post and comment upvoting.

Write path:
- User taps upvote → sign vote payload with Ed25519 key → AES-GCM encrypt with room key → write to Firestore
- Vote document: { sender_pubkey, target_id, type: 'up'|'important', sig, sent_at }

Read path:
- Subscribe to Firestore votes collection for visible posts
- Aggregate client-side: count unique sender_pubkeys per post
- Surface 'importance' votes separately — these float posts to Today's Highlights

'Important' is a distinct signal from 'upvote': marks something as critical news people need to see, not just popular.

title: Ed25519 identity — keypair generation and local storage
description:
Implement the zero-signup identity layer, shared concept across both apps.

macOS (Swift):
- Generate Ed25519 keypair on first launch via CryptoKit
- Store private key in Keychain (service: dev.MaroMushii.Khorshid)
- Public key is the user's identity — display name stored alongside it locally
- Export keypair as QR code for device migration

Android (Dart):
- Generate Ed25519 keypair using pointycastle or cryptography package
- Store in Android Keystore
- Same display name + public key model

No server-side registration. No GitHub account. No email.

title: Android app skeleton — Flutter + news feed from export branch
description:
Bootstrap the Flutter Android app under android/.

- flutter create with package name dev.MaroMushii.khorshid
- MirrorRepository — fetches index.json + snapshots from raw.githubusercontent.com
- NewsProvider (Riverpod) — channels + posts state
- Basic scaffold: channel list drawer + post feed
- Post card: channel name, headline, timestamp, media thumbnail
- Polling every 5 min
- No GT proxy needed on Android (direct raw.githubusercontent.com fetch is sufficient for MVP)

No social layer yet — read-only news feed.

title: macOS app skeleton — xcodegen + news feed from export branch
description:
Bootstrap the SwiftUI macOS 26 app under mac/.

- project.yml with xcodegen config (bundle ID: dev.MaroMushii.Khorshid)
- KhorshidApp.swift entry point
- MirrorClient — fetches index.json + channel snapshots from raw.githubusercontent.com/MaroMushii/Khorshid/refs/heads/export/
- ChannelStore (@Observable, @MainActor) — holds channels + posts
- Basic two-column NavigationSplitView: channel list left, post feed right
- Post row: channel name, headline, timestamp, view count, reactions
- Polling every 5 min (same cadence as mirror)
- GT proxy + pinned IPs for bypass (port from Pigeon's PinnedHTTPSClient + PinnedURLProtocol)

No social layer yet — read-only news feed.
notes: Started. Branch: feat/mac-app-skeleton. Plan: .beads/scratch/Khorshid-50o.md. GT proxy excluded (raw.githubusercontent.com whitelisted in Iran).

title: Design Firestore schema for comments and votes
description:
Define the Firestore collection structure for the social layer.

Collections needed:
- posts/{postId}/votes — per-post vote counts and voter pubkeys
- posts/{postId}/comments — encrypted comment blobs
- posts/{postId}/comments/{commentId}/votes — comment upvotes

Each document:
- signed with sender Ed25519 keypair
- AES-GCM encrypted with room key
- post_id references the Telegram post ID from the mirror snapshot (e.g. 'bbcpersian/1234')

Also spec out the daily aggregation format:
feed/{day}.json — posts sorted by vote count, written by GH Action
